approvalBoundary.summary: Live runtime implementation requires a later explicit approval record, host-policy gate, implementation plan, tests, and review checkpoint; this proposal cannot grant that approval.
requiredBeforeImplementation: explicit host-policy approval record naming the live runtime scope
operator consent for process stdio ownership and lifecycle control
Rust-host ownership of stdin/stdout/stderr lifecycle defined and tested
stdout JSONL framing contract enforced before any live stream is accepted
stderr diagnostic redaction policy enforced before bytes leave the process
transcript persistence and replay schema/version policy accepted
failure audit record schema and fixtures accepted
kill, exit, and fail-closed semantics accepted
backpressure and partial-write behavior tested with deterministic simulations
dropped, duplicate, out-of-order, and malformed-line behavior tested
negative tests prove runtime/proposal commands stay unavailable until implementation is separately approved

proposalSections.title: Approval Boundary
proposalSections.summary: Defines the exact gate before live runtime work: explicit host-policy approval, operator consent, scoped implementation, passing required tests, and a later runtime-readiness review.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: A separately approved implementation phase must create and validate an approval record before any live runtime command is added.

proposalSections.title: Rust-Host Stdio Ownership
proposalSections.summary: Future Rust host owns process stdio lifecycle, buffering, flushing, backpressure, termination, audit records, and redaction enforcement; TypeScript core remains contract-data only.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Rust-host ownership must be implemented behind explicit host policy in a later phase, with no TypeScript stdin loop or process supervisor.

proposalSections.title: Stdout JSONL Emission
proposalSections.summary: Future stdout is reserved for UTF-8 LF-only session-event JSONL, one complete schema-valid event per line, contiguous sequence values, final LF, and no diagnostics.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Live stdout writer tests must prove whole-line commits, schema validation, ordering, uniqueness, final LF, and zero diagnostic leakage.

proposalSections.title: Stderr Diagnostics And Redaction
proposalSections.summary: Future stderr is reserved for deterministic diagnostics only; secrets, tokens, raw environment dumps, stack traces, absolute local paths, usernames, and production keys must be redacted or suppressed.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Redaction enforcement must be active and tested before live stderr can emit runtime diagnostics.

proposalSections.title: Transcript Persistence And Replay
proposalSections.summary: Future persistence should store normalized ardyn.session-transcript JSON plus inert capture metadata; replay consumes validated normalized transcripts, not raw process bytes.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Persistence and replay require schema/version fixtures, compatibility classification, and negative tests before any replay command is implemented.

proposalSections.title: Failure Audit Records
proposalSections.summary: Future host failures should create deterministic review records for transport, redaction, backpressure, line-integrity, approval denial, and internal runtime failure classes.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Audit record schema, fixtures, and fail-closed mapping tests must exist before runtime implementation starts.

proposalSections.title: Kill, Exit, And Fail-Closed Semantics
proposalSections.summary: Future success exits require a complete valid terminal state; failures exit nonzero, avoid partial final stdout lines, flush safe diagnostics, and never count partial events as transcript evidence.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Exit/kill simulations must prove terminal-state success, nonzero failure classes, no partial commits, and deterministic cleanup.

proposalSections.title: Backpressure And Partial Writes
proposalSections.summary: Future host writes must respect OS pipe backpressure, bound queues or terminate explicitly, use a full JSONL line as the smallest commit unit, and never drop events silently.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Backpressure and partial-write tests must simulate blocked consumers, bounded queues, whole-line commits, and reject/terminate behavior.

proposalSections.title: Dropped, Duplicate, Out-Of-Order, And Malformed Lines
proposalSections.summary: Future transport treats sequence gaps, duplicates, out-of-order events, invalid JSON, non-object JSON, schema-invalid events, CRLF, blank lines, unsafe safety flags, non-ARDYN harness, and command-like root fields as invalid.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Line-integrity tests must prove fail-closed classification without autocorrection, synthesis, downgrade, or replay recovery unless a later versioned policy defines it.

proposalSections.title: Required Tests Before Runtime
proposalSections.summary: Defines the mandatory future test families that must pass before any live stdio runtime path can exist.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: A later implementation phase must include these tests as blocking gates, not optional review notes.

proposalSections.title: Phased Runtime Roadmap
proposalSections.summary: Defines an ordered future implementation roadmap without implementing any runtime behavior in Phase 4.1.
proposalSections.requiredOutcomeBeforeRuntime: Each future roadmap phase requires explicit approval, implementation, tests, and review before enabling its runtime surface.

requiredTestsBeforeRuntime.summary: Tests must prove no live runtime starts without an explicit host-policy approval record and operator consent.
Rust tests must prove the host owns stdio lifecycle, buffering, flushing, backpressure, termination, and audit emission before live stdio is enabled.
Tests must prove stdout is LF-only session-event JSONL with one complete schema-valid event per line, contiguous sequence values, final LF, and no diagnostics.
Tests must prove stderr diagnostics redact secrets, tokens, raw environments, stack traces, absolute paths, usernames, and production keys.
Tests must prove normalized transcript persistence and replay validation remain inert and reject malformed, duplicate, dropped, and out-of-order evidence.
Tests must pin deterministic failure audit records for transport, redaction, backpressure, line-integrity, host-policy denial, and internal failure classes.
Tests must prove exit 0 requires complete terminal state and every failure class exits nonzero without committing partial events.
Tests must simulate blocked consumers, bounded queues, whole-line commit units, partial-write rejection, and explicit termination behavior.
Tests must reject dropped, duplicate, out-of-order, malformed, CRLF, blank, unsafe, non-ARDYN, and command-like event lines without autocorrection.
Tests must prove proposal and runtime command names remain unavailable until a later approved implementation phase adds them deliberately.

implementationRoadmap.summary: Define and test host-policy approval records, operator consent fields, runtime scope names, denial reasons, and review-only display behavior.
Implement Rust-host stdio lifecycle harness behind explicit approval gates with deterministic fake-process tests before any real process ownership.
Implement stdout JSONL whole-line writer and stderr redaction enforcement with blocked-consumer and partial-write tests.
Implement normalized transcript persistence and inert replay review after schema, compatibility, redaction, and failure-audit tests exist.
Implement deterministic failure audit records, kill handling, cleanup, terminal-state checks, and nonzero exit mapping.
Run the major runtime-readiness checkpoint, including full Codex validation and the preserved Devin review, before enabling any live runtime surface.
